name: Hendrell Remus
subject: Republican Rep Sherrell said what??
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee Democratic Party
body:
Peter,

Today, the House Criminal Justice Committee met to discuss adding firing squads to electrocution and lethal injection as Tennessee's death penalty options, when Republican Representative Paul Sherrell suggested another [https://www.thedailybeast.com/tennessee-lawmaker-paul-sherrell-suggests-hanging-on-a-tree-as-execution-method] :

"Could I put an amendment on that that would include hanging on a tree, also?"

Lynching — that's what Paul wants to bring back. The racist nature of this comment becomes even more sinister when we consider the history of Sparta, Tennessee, which Sherrell represents, where enslaved people were lynched and hung by trees for running away.
This is what we're up against. The bold racism from Republicans like Paul is getting worse — we've got to put a stop to it.

Can you chip in $20 now to help fight against Republican racism by electing more Democrats to the state legislature? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
$5 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=express_lane_5]
$10 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
$25 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
$50 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50]
$100 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
OTHER » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]

You just can’t make this stuff up. Republican control has turned into a full assault on human decency. Advocating for lynching is not an accident — Sherrell knows what he wants, and what he wants is white supremacy. We've got to fight back.

But we need your help — can we count on your support in our fight to elect more Democrats to the state legislature so we can oust racist Republicans like Paul? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

Yours in the fight,

Hendrell Remus
he/him
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

Paid for by the Tennessee Democratic Party.Carol V. Abney, Treasurer.

name: Ro Khanna
subject: We need to cap prices on life-saving medications
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
Thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act that Congress passed in 2022, vital medication is becoming more affordable.
#
Friend,
The cost of life-saving prescription medicine in America is out of control. According to a [poll](https://act.rokhanna.com/go/11298?t=7&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j) by Kaiser Family Health, eight in ten adults say that the cost of prescription drugs is unreasonable. And almost 30 percent aren’t taking their medication as prescribed due to cost.
**Thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act that Congress passed in 2022, vital medication is becoming more affordable.** For the first time, Medicare has the power to negotiate prescription drug prices. Insulin costs have been capped at $35 per month for Medicare patients as a result.
Now, Eli Lilly has [announced](https://act.rokhanna.com/go/11299?t=8&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j) they’ll be capping out-of-pocket costs for insulin at $35 a month for everyone.
Make no mistake, Eli Lilly would not have taken these steps without pressure from the public and the federal government to make insulin more affordable. This is a huge step towards lowering the cost of a prescription drug that millions rely on to survive. But other pharmaceutical companies continue to inflate prices for profit.
[**Sign the petition: Tell pharmaceutical companies to follow Eli Lilly’s lead and cap the cost of insulin at $35 a month.**](https://act.rokhanna.com/sign/cap-insulin-prices-petition/?t=9&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j)
Onward,
Ro

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ro-em-footer?refcode=em20230301-2635-footer&t=10&refcode2=2635_946728_a3uB2j&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j)**
---
[](https://facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA) | [](https://twitter.com/RoKhanna)
Email us: [info@rokhanna.com](mailto: info@rokhanna.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Ro for Congress, PO Box 3513, Santa Clara, CA 95051.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.rokhanna.com/signup/update-your-information/?t=11&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.rokhanna.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=12&akid=2635%2E946728%2Ea3uB2j)

name: Doug Moore
subject: Announcement from OLCV Executive Director
body:
| | | Dear Members:

I wanted to let you know that I’ve decided to step down as OLCV Executive Director to pursue a new opportunity. I know, it’s big news and a big change for OLCV. But in life, sometimes it’s good to leave when you’re at the top of your game and make way for a graceful, smooth transition.

When I applied for this job in 2011, I said in my cover letter that the opportunity to lead OLCV would be a dream come true. I can say that it has been that and more. This has been the best job I’ve ever had, and I’m incredibly thankful for the opportunity, the support from members like you, and the friendship of all the great people who have worked at OLCV, as well as our many partners and allies. Leading this organization has been an honor and the highlight of my career, and while leaving is bittersweet, I’m also excited for my future as well as the future of OLCV.

My goal in leading OLCV was always two-fold: first, to build political power for the environmental community, and to use that political power to dramatically move Oregon forward on climate change, as well as protecting clean air, our water, our forests, and Oregon’s unique quality of life.

Over the past decade, OLCV played a key role in electing Governors Kate Brown and Tina Kotek, winning back the Legislature, and building strong pro-environment majorities. And we translated that political power into strong policies. Oregon created marine reserves, took on toxics in children’s products, and protected the Elliott State Forest. On climate, Oregon created the strongest clean fuels program in the country and we were the first state to ban the production or import of coal power. In 2020, after two years of walkouts, Gov. Kate Brown issued an executive order on climate, directly regulating our carbon emissions and putting us on track to meet our 2050 greenhouse gas reduction goals. To top it off, in 2021, we passed legislation to move Oregon to 100% clean electricity by 2040.

We wouldn’t have done any of that without the hard work and support of our many partners and allies. But more importantly, we wouldn’t have done it without you. [I hope you’ll consider making a contribution to OLCV to help ensure that the organization remains strong and at the top of its game during this transition.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59470831/394357631/-1698714710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIzZTc2NTg1LTkxYjgtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9iTBUHoyoGvS9A749STRoH_IFAu5NuMB6k8KSq4TnLc=&emci=5d1f5fbc-89b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=b3e76585-91b8-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=2107230) Because as long as members like you continue to support it, OLCV will remain strong and continue to play our important role in the constellation of advocacy groups. I’ve appreciated your support for OLCV, and I hope you’ll continue – as I will – as an active supporter and contributor.

Transitions are always hard, but we’ve been planning for this day, and we have a plan in place for going forward. Our Deputy Director, Lindsey Scholten, will take the reins as our Interim Executive Director on April 1, 2023. I’ll move to the background to help with fundraising for our Annual Event, provide support and continuity during the transition process, and help our lobbying team with advice through the end of the Legislative Session. OLCV has an incredible team, and our work will continue through the transition.

I hope I’ll see many of you at the OLCV Annual Event on April 28, but if not, I’m sure I’ll see you in the future.

Yours,

Doug Moore

OLCV Executive Director |
---|---

name: Team Lucas Kunce
subject: NEW: GOP senators consider raising Social Security retirement age to almost 70
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
Here’s how Lucas would vote if he were in the U.S. Senate: Hell. No.
#
Peter — have you heard about the latest Senate proposal for Social Security?
**After months of threatening cuts to Social Security + Medicare in the House, a group of GOP senators now want to raise the retirement age to almost 70 years old.**
Here’s how Lucas would vote on that if he were in the U.S. Senate: **Hell. No.**
Everyday Americans spend their whole lives working to earn Social Security benefits — and they deserve leaders who are willing to stand up and fight to make sure they get every penny they’re owed.
**That’s why Lucas is running to defeat Josh Hawley and flip Missouri’s U.S. Senate seat, and it’s why he needs your help today.[Will you add a donation of $10 or whatever you can to our grassroots campaign so we can elect a real warrior for working people and protect the promise of Social Security?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/28141?refcode=em20230301-2842&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=2842%2E614464%2E5cstZ3)**

[](https://substack.com/redirect/3cc076be-c1ea-4073-bbeb-a6800679a4b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=105940536&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTA1OTQwNTM2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY3NzczMDkzNSwiZXhwIjoxNjgwMzIyOTM1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9X_Ikmgx3fRUEqSSI7bR_Zg8qJHfBppDoO3zDVx0P3c&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=105940536&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTA1OTQwNTM2LCJpYXQiOjE2Nzc3MzA5MzUsImV4cCI6MTY4MDMyMjkzNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wnw1nKCDwVJ1Vqag0C3Bsu9yV0hCEJ51Jd6WvQ9d000&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=105940536&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTA1OTQwNTM2LCJpYXQiOjE2Nzc3MzA5MzUsImV4cCI6MTY4MDMyMjkzNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wnw1nKCDwVJ1Vqag0C3Bsu9yV0hCEJ51Jd6WvQ9d000)
This week, _the Nation_ published [a quite thoughtful oped](https://substack.com/redirect/f0485abb-28cc-471f-87af-9af32bba77cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) assessing the profound threat to constitutional rights and freedom of conscience posed by illegal government surveillance. Author Patrick Toomey focused on the more or less total failure of the federal courts over the past generation to do their jobs in guarding constitutional rights from an executive branch run amok, prompted by the Supreme Court's recent decision to ignore [an appeal by the Wikimedia Foundation](https://substack.com/redirect/617a37f8-6c78-4f30-892c-15461c92fdb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) challenging illegal mass surveillance by the National Security Agency.
[T]he press can't be relied upon to guard or even recognize its own rights, let alone do the hard work of protecting democracy from government crimes committed in secret.
While I'm grateful for attention to an issue that has defined my career over the past two decades, it is remarkably convenient for a major publication to finally wake up and smell the coffee by publishing Toomey's oped in 2023, after having previously failed to cover congressional campaigns championing precisely the issues that editors now performatively claim to care about--only decades after the problem already grew entrenched.
[Ruth E. Kastner @rekastnerSo, @thenation, why exactly did you ignore the candidacy of @ShahidForChange, who supported virtually all (if not all) of your stated policies? It's a mystery to me. Please shed some light on this. Thanks. Shahid Buttar (find me on Substack) @ShahidForChange@thenation Having run for Congress on a platform to end illegal mass surveillance, only to see @thenation ignore the campaign and the historic challenge we presented to the establishment, it is striking to see how little editors appear to care about their own policy prescriptions. https://t.co/azjIXoKexv](https://substack.com/redirect/a59d9b20-20d6-44e4-906c-472e5262e07b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[5:03 AM ∙ Feb 27, 2023
* * *
41Likes8Retweets](https://substack.com/redirect/a59d9b20-20d6-44e4-906c-472e5262e07b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Worse yet, the press has abandoned not only its constitutional function, and public responsibility to expose government crimes (including mass surveillance and its many impacts on dissent and our communities), but in the process, abandoned constitutional rights both in the abstract, and even particularly those on which the press itself relies.
In other words, the press can't be relied upon to guard--or even recognize--its own rights, let alone do the hard work of protecting democracy from government crimes committed in secret.
In the end, it is democracy that suffers.
If you find this post informative, please consider subscribing to hear perspectives you won't find in corporate news outlets.
### Focusing on the courts while ignoring Congress
As far as Toomey's oped goes, I find little fault with it beyond its predictable underinclusion. It was written, after all, by an advocate from the ACLU, the American Civil Liberties Union. The organization has long challenged government surveillance programs, but remains institutionally focused on courts. Both the national network and its various state affiliates concentrate on impact litigation (filing lawsuits to impact public policy), with some work focused on elected legislatures, but little to none addressing political campaigns to challenge candidates or shift their policy positions.
Part of the ACLU's strategic commitment to courts is intrinsic to nonprofits generally. Under Section 501(c)(3) of the federal tax code, many exchange the ability to opine about electoral contests in exchange for the authority to offer tax deductibility to its donors. The ACLU and many of its state affiliates do work with sister organizations organized under Section 501(c)(4), which permits non-profit lobbying--but even they are restrained from engaging in electioneering.
Beyond its legal restrictions, the strategic commitment of organizations like the ACLU to impact litigation also reflects an unfortunate inability to adapt to the times.
The ACLU, like the NAACP Legal Defense Fund, won many victories in the mid-20th century, during an era when federal courts grew quite aggressive in responding to structural injustice. But in the decades since groundbreaking decisions like _Brown v Board_ (desegregating schools), _Batson v Kentucky_ (ending all white juries) and _Miranda v Arizona_ (ensuring notice of rights to people suspected of having committed a crime), our federal courts have been dramatically transformed through a combination of [weaponized appointments](https://substack.com/redirect/460e9102-cf9b-428b-9c3d-9df1334e87b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a pattern of "[Kabuki theater](https://substack.com/redirect/34b2644c-4d94-401d-ae51-d7f3dc2f3c38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" preventing the Senate from attaining meaningful oversight in confirmation hearings, a [disappointing failure](https://substack.com/redirect/b4019d14-9bcf-4953-be51-2d5b5766d69f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to retire by a liberal justice seemingly oblivious to the political context in which the courts operate, and the impunity of right wing jurists for [ethical violations](https://substack.com/redirect/4cde74bb-2012-491d-8df5-ebc1c152dbdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and professional failures both before and [after](https://substack.com/redirect/19541a6c-b277-4a04-949c-dd0c0ba1ef88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reaching the bench.
Just last year, the Supreme Court effectively reversed its historic _Miranda_ decision by [eliminating](https://substack.com/redirect/b395b084-6719-411f-820f-89238a8c8789?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) any remedy for government violations. It previously did the same thing to _Roe v Wade_ --[not in 2022, but in 2007](https://substack.com/redirect/788ef52c-eec6-4f64-9709-631d0443ec45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the same year it also substantially overruled _Brown v Board_ without admitting to doing so. While the Court assaulted its own precedents, non-profits committed to impact litigation played along, refusing to politicize the increasingly political Court out of fear of alienating Justices before whom they would have to argue in the future.
All that is to say: it makes sense for an ACLU employee to write an oped focused on the courts, since that's where organization focuses (whether or not that strategy makes sense anymore).
But there's no good reason for editors to privilege analysis of the courts over observations about political campaigns that aim to place these issues in courts of public opinion. That's especially true since the courts have proven so absent, and the political branches so thoroughly complicit across both corporate parties.
[I could go on a tangent here about the structural inability of courts to show up for work, having previously marginalized themselves--but I'll save that for later. Paid subscribers with an interest in digging deeper can read that analysis below, behind the paywall.]
Want to learn more about the roots--and many branches--of bipartisan corruption in Washington?
### Mass surveillance offends democracy--and your rights
Mass surveillance is constitutionally troubling for many reasons, not the least of which is that it violates the Fourth Amendment right against unreasonable searches and seizures. Courts have [long required](https://substack.com/redirect/82821753-e167-4edf-b640-0542ef604f5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) police, for instance, to establish at least reasonable suspicion of a criminal offense before even so much as simply patting someone down for weapons--yet authorities have claimed access to the most private thoughts, correspondence, and financial transactions of hundreds of millions of Americans (and billions of others beyond us) without any basis of suspicion at all.
The fault lies with corruption in government, and the many actors who enable it....
Many have characterized this erosion of rights as "technology outpacing the law," which is both foolish and charitable. It is true that technology enabled our government to weaponize its powers and circumvent previous legal restrictions, but the technology has been, as it always is, nothing more than a tool.
The fault lies with corruption in government, and the many actors who enable it--from [insider trading Members of Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/7343612f-6210-4f38-9eb8-2d42aa28e2af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who place their private fortunes before the public interest, to the [complicit editors of publications](https://substack.com/redirect/1883d3cf-b528-4204-9315-d6d20c79318d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who insulate them from public accountability.
The unconstitutional mass surveillance regime has also eroded First Amendment rights and freedom of conscience, belief, association, and expression. We'll come back to that in a minute.
### Corruption hiding in plain sight
Apologists for mass surveillance claim that it has been focused exclusively on ensuring national security. The evidence, however, suggests otherwise: the NSA has [caught](https://substack.com/redirect/fd933f97-34be-42ed-a5b2-b85bfe9ca45d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an untold number of agents misusing the government's powerful domestic spying tools not to track national security threats, but instead to monitor their former lovers or spouses.
Meanwhile, the specter of unaccountable government openly [politicizing](https://substack.com/redirect/332a40e9-6184-4af7-a7cd-990044709b15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the surveillance apparatus renders it only as trustworthy as the figures at the top of (or, frankly, anywhere within) the executive branch.
Given the recent history of the presidency, there is absolutely no good reason for anyone to feel any degree of assurance that the system will not be used for the craven and corrupt purposes of advancing someone's political agenda, or even perhaps the fortune of a self-interested public official. Enlarging their private fortunes does, after all, seem to be the one thing that unites Democrats & Republicans in Washington.
### The press won't even stand up for itself, let alone democracy
Another reason that surveillance is constitutionally offensive is that it undermines the right of association protected by the First Amendment. Rather than merely protect speech, as many think, the First Amendment actually protects five distinct political rights (belief, association, speech, publication, and advocacy), which together comprise what NYU law professor Brett Neuborne describes as "[Madison's Music](https://substack.com/redirect/299bd847-ac67-45a6-b1b8-905c24fb6f65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Editors...have routinely ignored advocates and whistleblowers--in addition to congressional candidates who have stood with them and constitutional rights--while choosing to instead privilege self-serving government sources to construct an artificial veneer of bipartisan legitimacy surrounding programs that have never enjoyed any basis for legality.
Alas, the Supreme Court ([in 1976](https://substack.com/redirect/fe12c346-4f4f-46da-bb69-39b3b8e72af6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and then [again in 2010](https://substack.com/redirect/66b12074-3382-4d1e-b705-16eeb102681b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) made the indefensible decision to treat political campaign contributions like speech, undermining the founders' vision of political process rights by turning our democracy over to the highest (usually, a corporate) bidder.
But even setting aside the Supreme Court's abandonment of the First Amendment's purposes in its jurisprudence [explored more deeply in the 2012 article to which I link after the paywall below], it is unfortunately clear from the record that even editors have abandoned their own rights, long before ever facing judges.
The right of association, in particular, has vital importance to news organizations that rely on source anonymity to gain access to information from whistleblowers and government officials who are not authorized to speak on a public record. Because government surveillance renders whistleblowers at ever increasing risk of exposure, news organizations have lost a pipeline to vital sources of information.
Editors recognize this problem, so much so that they have taken steps through technology (more so than their journalism) to respond. The [Secure Drop](https://substack.com/redirect/bc3a27a8-1d23-4f21-a368-30d93211a3d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) system developed by the [Freedom of the Press Foundation](https://substack.com/redirect/b162383f-1ac1-40a1-bc97-6547cf625041?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has offered a critical tool for investigative journalists to offer continuing anonymity to sources who wish not to be identified.
But despite embracing technology to address their vulnerability to government monitoring, most publications and news organizations have completely abdicated the parallel responsibility to inform the public about why they go to such lengths, or why such lengths matter at all in the broader context of defending democracy from demagoguery.
There have been no shortage of opportunities for the press to show up. Concerns about illegal mass surveillance have emerged from [both sides](https://substack.com/redirect/f94e1ec2-07c7-467f-a3b8-922e31493c75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the [partisan aisle](https://substack.com/redirect/198d7f00-802d-4e0b-8bef-a0c08758ee0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and prompted reform campaigns at the local and state level across the country, in addition to closely contested federal campaigns like the one I mounted in 2020.
Editors, however, have routinely ignored advocates and whistleblowers--in addition to congressional candidates who have stood with them and constitutional rights--while choosing to instead privilege self-serving government sources to construct an artificial veneer of bipartisan legitimacy surrounding programs that have never enjoyed any basis for legality.
Obvious questions have gone unasked by journalists, leaving to activists and advocates the job abandoned by editors. [My arrest in the U.S. Senate in 2015](https://substack.com/redirect/90546be4-34d5-4ffb-89d2-7d3379a9b740?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was prompted by this pattern: I asked an important question (as it happens, about impunity for illegal mass surveillance, juxtaposed with the relentless, so-called "justice" that leaves innocent Americans with dark skin dead at the hands of paid public servants with disturbing frequency) that no journalist has ever demonstrated the independence to ask any public official anywhere in the country.
Writing in _the Nation_ , Toomey fairly bemoans the failures of federal judges to intervene. Several judges, however, have issued scathing rulings attempting to rein in the surveillance state, only to be overruled by superior courts, ultimately beholden to the authoritarian consensus that defines the current majority of the Supreme Court.
The crux of their deference has been the [state secrets doctrine](https://substack.com/redirect/5eccbc5b-e37f-4d56-819f-29a261be4876?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a judicially contrived rule announced in a case later [revealed](https://substack.com/redirect/342f011b-8446-4a63-965c-c3cb9d0c4eef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to be a quintessential military cover-up that has allowed the White House in recent years to suppress public knowledge of everything from mass [surveillance](https://substack.com/redirect/81e2a77b-1e86-4bfc-a2f9-e73e0538f3c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the CIA's role in international human rights abuses including [torture](https://substack.com/redirect/7b69997c-4cba-43c1-93fe-1aea8baaaf2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with impunity, the FBI's [infiltrating](https://substack.com/redirect/856faff5-0e22-4c0f-a7dc-c8f46ba652b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) mosques around the country, and God only knows what else.
Observing the continuing rightward shift in American jurisprudence, many have suggested that elections have consequences. That is precisely why I feel compelled to explain the institutional failure of press outlets across the United States, including _the Nation_ and nearly every news organization in this country. The only hope for a better jurisprudence and more defensible policymaking is for the press to finally start informing the public about the bipartisan corruption that provides Washington.
But when editors of major publications address vital issues only decades after the fact, after having ignored political candidates who championed those issues in real time, they reduce themselves to entertainment.
### Democracy in crisis
Americans pretend that our media ecosystem is more trustworthy than those in countries in which the outlets are formally state owned, but between corporate consolidation, access journalism, and deference to government secrecy, our supposedly "Free Press" has little credibility left.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e2c6b965-6bdd-450c-8647-2edebe3495af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The recurring exposure of government lies after the fact of every armed conflict initiated by the Pentagon over the last three generations is reason enough to be skeptical of today's [escalating warmongering](https://substack.com/redirect/b98fcdbc-8234-4ee6-a619-ac0e1bbcb462?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Washington. Our military industrial complex is currently stoking conflict vis-a-vis two nuclear armed powers at the same time--yet rather than offer any independent analysis, media outlets in the U.S. have yet again played their reliable roles as cheerleaders for war.
I'm reminded of how Malcolm X responded after he was neutralized (but before he was killed), by going on an international tour to expose the contradictions and illegitimacy of America's position in the international arena, claiming to stand for democracy and human rights while denying basic rights to many of its own citizens. I see in Brother Malcolm's example a lesson to seek new arenas in which to challenge the foundations of those that we were forced to abandon. Having been pushed out of electoral politics by the racist corruption and political co-optation of editors & journalists, I am eager to expose their complicity by doing the important work they unfortunately abandoned.
Assessing the credibility of voices who claim to care about principles only after the fact, and only in moments when they are not politically contested, is a crucial component of accountability. The first step to defending our democracy is to hold accountable the editors whose failures have grown so unfortunately routine.
By reading independent voices that offer the analysis and perspective you won't find from news organizations, you're doing that right now.
Thank you for informing yourself by examining a dissident voice reading between the lines! This post is public, so feel free to share it with others who could learn from this analysis.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=105940536&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTA1OTQwNTM2LCJpYXQiOjE2Nzc3MzA5MzUsImV4cCI6MTY4MDMyMjkzNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wnw1nKCDwVJ1Vqag0C3Bsu9yV0hCEJ51Jd6WvQ9d000)
Paid subscribers can learn more from a keynote speech I delivered at a law symposium in Chicago about a decade ago. It addresses a wide range of themes, including the illegality of CIA drone strikes and unreliability of even the Obama administration's supposedly limiting principles. It also touches on the marginalization of courts at the hands of judges who, like their counterparts in the press, routinely defer to executive secrecy, abandoning human rights and accountability in the process...

The Nation's editors embrace performative "journalism" | | | ---|---|--- | | Shahid Buttar| | | | | | This week, the Nation published assessing the profound threat to constitutional rights and freedom of conscience posed by illegal government surveillance. Author Patrick Toomey focused on the more or less total failure of the federal courts over the past generation to do their jobs in guarding constitutional rights from an executive branch run amok, prompted by the Supreme Court's recent decision to ignore challenging illegal mass surveillance by the National Security Agency. [T]he press can't be relied upon to guard or even recognize its own rights, let alone do the hard work of protecting democracy from government crimes committed in secret. While I'm grateful for attention to an issue that has defined my career over the past two decades, it is remarkably convenient for a major publication to finally wake up and smell the coffee by publishing Toomey's oped in 2023, after having previously failed to cover congressional campaigns championing precisely the issues that editors now performatively claim to care about--only decades after the problem already grew entrenched. Worse yet, the press has abandoned not only its constitutional function, and public responsibility to expose government crimes (including mass surveillance and its many impacts on dissent and our communities), but in the process, abandoned constitutional rights both in the abstract, and even particularly those on which the press itself relies. In other words, the press can't be relied upon to guard--or even recognize--its own rights, let alone do the hard work of protecting democracy from government crimes committed in secret. In the end, it is democracy that suffers. If you find this post informative, please consider subscribing to hear perspectives you won't find in corporate news outlets. Focusing on the courts while ignoring Congress As far as Toomey's oped goes, I find little fault with it beyond its predictable underinclusion. It was written, after all, by an advocate from the ACLU, the American Civil Liberties Union. The organization has long challenged government surveillance programs, but remains institutionally focused on courts. Both the national network and its various state affiliates concentrate on impact litigation (filing lawsuits to impact public policy), with some work focused on elected legislatures, but little to none addressing political campaigns to challenge candidates or shift their policy positions. Part of the ACLU's strategic commitment to courts is intrinsic to nonprofits generally. Under Section 501(c)(3) of the federal tax code, many exchange the ability to opine about electoral contests in exchange for the authority to offer tax deductibility to its donors. The ACLU and many of its state affiliates do work with sister organizations organized under Section 501(c)(4), which permits non-profit lobbying--but even they are restrained from engaging in electioneering. Beyond its legal restrictions, the strategic commitment of organizations like the ACLU to impact litigation also reflects an unfortunate inability to adapt to the times. The ACLU, like the NAACP Legal Defense Fund, won many victories in the mid-20th century, during an era when federal courts grew quite aggressive in responding to structural injustice. But in the decades since groundbreaking decisions like Brown v Board (desegregating schools), Batson v Kentucky (ending all white juries) and Miranda v Arizona (ensuring notice of rights to people suspected of having committed a crime), our federal courts have been dramatically transformed through a combination of , a pattern of "" preventing the Senate from attaining meaningful oversight in confirmation hearings, a to retire by a liberal justice seemingly oblivious to the political context in which the courts operate, and the impunity of right wing jurists for and professional failures both before and reaching the bench. Just last year, the Supreme Court effectively reversed its historic Miranda decision by any remedy for government violations. It previously did the same thing to Roe v Wade --, the same year it also substantially overruled Brown v Board without admitting to doing so. While the Court assaulted its own precedents, non-profits committed to impact litigation played along, refusing to politicize the increasingly political Court out of fear of alienating Justices before whom they would have to argue in the future. All that is to say: it makes sense for an ACLU employee to write an oped focused on the courts, since that's where organization focuses (whether or not that strategy makes sense anymore). But there's no good reason for editors to privilege analysis of the courts over observations about political campaigns that aim to place these issues in courts of public opinion. That's especially true since the courts have proven so absent, and the political branches so thoroughly complicit across both corporate parties. [I could go on a tangent here about the structural inability of courts to show up for work, having previously marginalized themselves--but I'll save that for later. Paid subscribers with an interest in digging deeper can read that analysis below, behind the paywall.] Want to learn more about the roots--and many branches--of bipartisan corruption in Washington? Mass surveillance offends democracy--and your rights Mass surveillance is constitutionally troubling for many reasons, not the least of which is that it violates the Fourth Amendment right against unreasonable searches and seizures. Courts have police, for instance, to establish at least reasonable suspicion of a criminal offense before even so much as simply patting someone down for weapons--yet authorities have claimed access to the most private thoughts, correspondence, and financial transactions of hundreds of millions of Americans (and billions of others beyond us) without any basis of suspicion at all. The fault lies with corruption in government, and the many actors who enable it.... Many have characterized this erosion of rights as "technology outpacing the law," which is both foolish and charitable. It is true that technology enabled our government to weaponize its powers and circumvent previous legal restrictions, but the technology has been, as it always is, nothing more than a tool. The fault lies with corruption in government, and the many actors who enable it--from who place their private fortunes before the public interest, to the who insulate them from public accountability. The unconstitutional mass surveillance regime has also eroded First Amendment rights and freedom of conscience, belief, association, and expression. We'll come back to that in a minute. Corruption hiding in plain sight Apologists for mass surveillance claim that it has been focused exclusively on ensuring national security. The evidence, however, suggests otherwise: the NSA has an untold number of agents misusing the government's powerful domestic spying tools not to track national security threats, but instead to monitor their former lovers or spouses. Meanwhile, the specter of unaccountable government openly the surveillance apparatus renders it only as trustworthy as the figures at the top of (or, frankly, anywhere within) the executive branch. Given the recent history of the presidency, there is absolutely no good reason for anyone to feel any degree of assurance that the system will not be used for the craven and corrupt purposes of advancing someone's political agenda, or even perhaps the fortune of a self-interested public official. Enlarging their private fortunes does, after all, seem to be the one thing that unites Democrats & Republicans in Washington. The press won't even stand up for itself, let alone democracy Another reason that surveillance is constitutionally offensive is that it undermines the right of association protected by the First Amendment. Rather than merely protect speech, as many think, the First Amendment actually protects five distinct political rights (belief, association, speech, publication, and advocacy), which together comprise what NYU law professor Brett Neuborne describes as "." Editors...have routinely ignored advocates and whistleblowers--in addition to congressional candidates who have stood with them and constitutional rights--while choosing to instead privilege self-serving government sources to construct an artificial veneer of bipartisan legitimacy surrounding programs that have never enjoyed any basis for legality. Alas, the Supreme Court ( and then ) made the indefensible decision to treat political campaign contributions like speech, undermining the founders' vision of political process rights by turning our democracy over to the highest (usually, a corporate) bidder. But even setting aside the Supreme Court's abandonment of the First Amendment's purposes in its jurisprudence [explored more deeply in the 2012 article to which I link after the paywall below], it is unfortunately clear from the record that even editors have abandoned their own rights, long before ever facing judges. The right of association, in particular, has vital importance to news organizations that rely on source anonymity to gain access to information from whistleblowers and government officials who are not authorized to speak on a public record. Because government surveillance renders whistleblowers at ever increasing risk of exposure, news organizations have lost a pipeline to vital sources of information. Editors recognize this problem, so much so that they have taken steps through technology (more so than their journalism) to respond. The system developed by the has offered a critical tool for investigative journalists to offer continuing anonymity to sources who wish not to be identified. But despite embracing technology to address their vulnerability to government monitoring, most publications and news organizations have completely abdicated the parallel responsibility to inform the public about why they go to such lengths, or why such lengths matter at all in the broader context of defending democracy from demagoguery. There have been no shortage of opportunities for the press to show up. Concerns about illegal mass surveillance have emerged from of the , and prompted reform campaigns at the local and state level across the country, in addition to closely contested federal campaigns like the one I mounted in 2020. Editors, however, have routinely ignored advocates and whistleblowers--in addition to congressional candidates who have stood with them and constitutional rights--while choosing to instead privilege self-serving government sources to construct an artificial veneer of bipartisan legitimacy surrounding programs that have never enjoyed any basis for legality. Obvious questions have gone unasked by journalists, leaving to activists and advocates the job abandoned by editors. was prompted by this pattern: I asked an important question (as it happens, about impunity for illegal mass surveillance, juxtaposed with the relentless, so-called "justice" that leaves innocent Americans with dark skin dead at the hands of paid public servants with disturbing frequency) that no journalist has ever demonstrated the independence to ask any public official anywhere in the country. Writing in the Nation , Toomey fairly bemoans the failures of federal judges to intervene. Several judges, however, have issued scathing rulings attempting to rein in the surveillance state, only to be overruled by superior courts, ultimately beholden to the authoritarian consensus that defines the current majority of the Supreme Court. The crux of their deference has been the , a judicially contrived rule announced in a case later to be a quintessential military cover-up that has allowed the White House in recent years to suppress public knowledge of everything from mass to the CIA's role in international human rights abuses including with impunity, the FBI's mosques around the country, and God only knows what else. Observing the continuing rightward shift in American jurisprudence, many have suggested that elections have consequences. That is precisely why I feel compelled to explain the institutional failure of press outlets across the United States, including the Nation and nearly every news organization in this country. The only hope for a better jurisprudence and more defensible policymaking is for the press to finally start informing the public about the bipartisan corruption that provides Washington. But when editors of major publications address vital issues only decades after the fact, after having ignored political candidates who championed those issues in real time, they reduce themselves to entertainment. Democracy in crisis Americans pretend that our media ecosystem is more trustworthy than those in countries in which the outlets are formally state owned, but between corporate consolidation, access journalism, and deference to government secrecy, our supposedly "Free Press" has little credibility left. | | ---|---|--- The recurring exposure of government lies after the fact of every armed conflict initiated by the Pentagon over the last three generations is reason enough to be skeptical of today's in Washington. Our military industrial complex is currently stoking conflict vis-a-vis two nuclear armed powers at the same time--yet rather than offer any independent analysis, media outlets in the U.S. have yet again played their reliable roles as cheerleaders for war. I'm reminded of how Malcolm X responded after he was neutralized (but before he was killed), by going on an international tour to expose the contradictions and illegitimacy of America's position in the international arena, claiming to stand for democracy and human rights while denying basic rights to many of its own citizens. I see in Brother Malcolm's example a lesson to seek new arenas in which to challenge the foundations of those that we were forced to abandon. Having been pushed out of electoral politics by the racist corruption and political co-optation of editors & journalists, I am eager to expose their complicity by doing the important work they unfortunately abandoned. Assessing the credibility of voices who claim to care about principles only after the fact, and only in moments when they are not politically contested, is a crucial component of accountability. The first step to defending our democracy is to hold accountable the editors whose failures have grown so unfortunately routine. By reading independent voices that offer the analysis and perspective you won't find from news organizations, you're doing that right now. Thank you for informing yourself by examining a dissident voice reading between the lines! This post is public, so feel free to share it with others who could learn from this analysis. Paid subscribers can learn more from a keynote speech I delivered at a law symposium in Chicago about a decade ago. It addresses a wide range of themes, including the illegality of CIA drone strikes and unreliability of even the Obama administration's supposedly limiting principles. It also touches on the marginalization of courts at the hands of judges who, like their counterparts in the press, routinely defer to executive secrecy, abandoning human rights and accountability in the process... name: Vivek Ramaswamy
subject: What does it mean to be an American in 2023?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Vivek 2024
body:
This dream has always stuck with me.

| [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zpnncs2onlpkig9g1bgahjuo2atmpb0lf65cf6d0_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)
Fellow American,

I am lucky. I grew up in Ohio in the '90s back when America still valued merit.

I might have been a skinny kid with a funny last name, but my parents taught me an important lesson: _If you are going to stand out, you might as well be outstanding._

If I'd been born 20 years later, my story would have been impossible. Today our nation is hungry for a cause; we want purpose and meaning.

The woke Left preys on that vacuum with a poisonous obsession with race, sexuality, and climate change. If you disagree with them, they call you a bigot. This is psychological slavery.

Fellow American, the best measure of the health of American democracy is the percentage of citizens who feel free to say what they actually think - _**IN PUBLIC.**_

Right now, we're failing that test.

**That's why I am running for President of the United States.**

[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zul9f1b5vikaatpjabnuri3mdbu6r01te3fafpjo_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)
Today, we're starting a cultural movement in our country, Fellow American, and I want you to be a part of it. I want you to join our movement to create a new American dream for the next generation.

This time it isn't just about money. It is about the unapologetic pursuit of excellence itself. It means that we:

Believe in merit.
Believe in accountability.
Believe in free speech.
Believe in American exceptionalism.
Fellow American, if you're on board with these ideals, then we're already on the same team. We're ready to fight for the future of America. If we rediscover our shared national identity, then we can take on the greatest external threat we face: Communist China.

**[Will you join me? Please take just a few minutes to watch my launch video- and join Team Vivek by chipping in $1 or even $5 today as I launch my presidential campaign.](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z0od84n3kq0dakfr6p210b9h864r1c3me4eh0kk8_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)**

It's time to stand up to the ruling aristocracy in America – in Washington D.C., on Wall Street, and in Silicon Valley – that does whatever allows them to accumulate more money and power for themselves. They try to divide & conquer, but that won't work if we stay united.

**E pluribus unum - _from many, one._**

These are the ideals that won the American Revolution; that reunited us after the Civil War; that won two World Wars and the Cold War. I believe deep in my bones these ideals still exist, and I am running for President to revive them.

Join me,

[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z0i05ni0od6d6r5vkr71pp3dmke2810i6shvdcfg_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)
Vivek Ramaswamy

| [** WATCH NOW ->**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zho7ma0lime5advb5504cl72cr8uald1pnesa000_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)
---

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

[** STAND WITH VIVEK ➞ **](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z2qar8nd3focf063o2apfnkqlafu60f2r5b5lueg_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)

---

| [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zssn9qtckiekna9sr336ech7pbojrju2hsn2hh3o_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)| [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z6pcftra0859hls9ued3gk7usrcspnic09a0a8h0_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)| [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z48g4kc13ffc6bftu8t39rog96q6efr8ijdv54j8_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh2bs)
---|---|---

| Paid for by Vivek 2024
---

Funds received in response to this solicitation are subject to federal contribution limits. Contributions from corporations, labor unions, foreign nationals without green cards, and federal government contractors are prohibited. [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zdgrrcoipg1srm113n088pgi2i3jieqpgpc0sqpo_rp22sh2s8i66pr7ctim2o71chh238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

# **Biden’s Sneaky H.R. 5376 Is A Nightmare For 401(k) And IRA Savers**
---
### **Biden’s Sneaky H.R. 5376 Is A Nightmare For 401(k) And IRA Savers**

| | | Hi, Dr. Ron Paul here.

Biden just made the lives of American retirement savers much harder.

_And the mainstream media is ignoring this story!_

**Here’s what happened:**

Joe Biden couldn’t get his “Build Back Better” bill passed last year because everyone knew it would cause **more inflation**. So he stubbornly pushed it through Washington in “H.R. 5376”.

Without a doubt, this sneaky move confirms what I’ve always believed:
* Governments will never stop printing fiat currency until it is worthless!
* Anyone keeping their savings in dollars or stocks should consider smarter options, TODAY!

---

YES! Physical gold offers an opportunity to **escape wealth confiscation**. And the best way to protect your savings with gold is through a PHYSICAL GOLD IRA.

2. The gold is in PHYSICAL form. Which is far more trustworthy than "paper" gold.
3. You _**won’t pay a DIME in penalties or taxes**_ when you sell your stocks and buy gold.

This is the perfect scenario if you are approaching or are already in retirement.

body:
Peter, Over the past few days, I've been telling you about the dangers of pop-up car rallies—which are more like riots that b
| [](https://xtz223.keap-link002.com/v2/click/ce2d87d1d66356d610976f10de8fe556/eJyNj00LgkAURf_LW0viR1DuREREcxG1jsl51JCOw_iUTPzvjRW1KWj73rnnckcglExSyiGAK91c1wMLNJZCCZQUNZJY-fi6S9_xHAsqIS-JbjoFwfg1_Abms79aW0CDQkPstmGUpUVyyNMiM6Bi2lT8p_E_mngTpjlM008v1oLi3qhbCEh3OO_hwmyiva4MfyZSbWDbZVPXjTyZDskXZdUd7Ur0aARMKZT8tT3D4amZ7ozTYoo=)
---
| Peter, Over the past few days, I've been telling you about the dangers of pop-up car rallies—which are more like riots that bring violence and harm to local NJ communities, small businesses and families. **I'll be going live for another episode of the Common Ground Podcast today at 4:30PM EST/1:30PM PST,** and I'll be covering some important topics with three special guests. Assemblyman Antawn McClellan will be joining me to talk about what we can do to stop pop-up violence, and the fatalities that have resulted from them.
# **[Click here to access the stream link for today's Common Ground episode directly](https://xtz223.keap-link002.com/v2/click/7557f4e9950790a06c7799f8e04a164f/eJyNj0ELgkAQhf_LnCXRjGpvIiKieYg6h7lDLem6rKNk4n9vrfBU0HXme9_jDUAoc0kxBwZ3erjuEizQWAglUFJQS8qL19ddec7SsaAU8hbpulXAhq_hGZjO3mZtAfUKDXHY-0ESZ9EpjbPEgCrXpuI_jbedNeHOj1MYx59erASFnVE3wEi3OO3hwmyioy4NfyVSDbPtoq6qWl5Mh-SLomzPdik6NIJcKZT8sz3B_q0Zn4s_Yog=) **(leave it open if you click early, I'll be live at 4:30PM EST). **To add the Common Ground podcast to your Google Calendar so you get notified every time we go live,[click here.](https://xtz223.keap-link002.com/v2/click/7fa6cc91f7e2c859ab83bcdb6fb225b4/eJyNj00LgkAQhv_LnCXxC8qbiIhoHqLOsa1LLem6rGNk4n9vrPBU0G2YeeZ5eUdAoZjCrIIQ7vhwXQ8sMIJLLYXCuFXI-OvqBr7jORbUUl1T0_YawvHr8wLMa38dWICDFkTsd1GcZ2V6LLIyJ1AzQxH_afzNokm2UVbANP30ikZiciN1ByGaXsx9Kkmd8GBq4i-Iugttm7dN06ozZahqxev-ZHNWC1UxQxKmNY2f_rkY3qrpCRlpZBA=)**
---
|
---
| If you haven't yet stood up for safety on our streets by filling out a quick form to alert your Legislators about the most common sense solution for car rallies, [**click here now (it's free!).**](https://xtz223.keap-link002.com/v2/click/b3eb70120623ab4da2677cafec31f717/eJyNj8EKgkAQQP9lzqK5GpQ3CRHRPESdY9GBFnVdtjEz8d9bKzwVdJ1584Y3AqHkkpISArjTgzEPLNBYCCVQ0q6VxIvXlq1913MtqIWsYt12CoLx6_ECzGN_41lAg0JDHA_hLk3y-JwleWpAxbV58Z_G3y6aaB8mGUzTTy82gqKbUV8hIN3h3FMK00QnXRv-QqQCx-n73iZeoekTrWQr5tlF25h7rhTK8pOe4vC2TE8EDWEw)
---
---
|
---
| I'm also going to be joined by Grassroots leader, Fran Deacon and School Board Member, Sal Piarulli, to cover a couple of additional critical issues. Fran is a local parental activist who is part of an eight-person local group called Citizens Defending Education (CDE), which is currently fighting to remove sexually graphic books and instructional sex guides aimed at children from New Jersey public libraries. Sal Piarulla is a School Board Member who is leading the charge in Garwood to resist and fight back against the Democrat’s plan to sexualize education. Sal is now under fire from the Board of Education, despite winning a decision in court that dismissed the Board's lawsuit against him. Antawn, Fran and Sal are excellent examples of people who are fighting the necessary fight to protect the citizens of New Jersey, including our innocent children who are being victimized by inappropriate indoctrination and sexualization forced into the classrooms. Join us for today's episode and help fight the good fight, Bill
# PS: If you don't want to miss today's Common Ground LIVE show, [click here to access the stream page now](https://xtz223.keap-link002.com/v2/click/c0ae0e8ddcd893277545d2da20cc3c58/eJyNj00LglAQRf_LrCXxIyh3IiKiuYhah-lQj_T5eI6Sif-9scJVQduZc8_ljkAoc0lxCR7c6WHbDhigsRBKoKSgkZQXr6-9di3HMqAS8hbpplPgjV_DCzCf3Q1HaFDIxGHvB0mcRac0zhIGVa654j-Nu1004c6PU5imn16sBYU9q1vwSHc47ykFb6Kjrpi_EqnWM82iqetGXrhDlqui6s5mJXpkQa4UyvKzPcHhrZmehoNigg==) and leave it open until we're live at 4:30PM ET!

clean_body: Peter, Over the past few days, I've been telling you about the dangers of pop-up car rallies—which are more like riots that b | = | Peter, Over the past few days, I've been telling you about the dangers of pop-up car rallies—which are more like riots that bring violence and harm to local NJ communities, small businesses and families. I'll be going live for another episode of the Common Ground Podcast today at 4:30PM EST/1:30PM PST, and I'll be covering some important topics with three special guests. Assemblyman Antawn McClellan will be joining me to talk about what we can do to stop pop-up violence, and the fatalities that have resulted from them. = (leave it open if you click early, I'll be live at 4:30PM EST). To add the Common Ground podcast to your Google Calendar so you get notified every time we go live,= | | If you haven't yet stood up for safety on our streets by filling out a quick form to alert your Legislators about the most common sense solution for car rallies, | | I'm also going to be joined by Grassroots leader, Fran Deacon and School Board Member, Sal Piarulli, to cover a couple of additional critical issues. Fran is a local parental activist who is part of an eight-person local group called Citizens Defending Education (CDE), which is currently fighting to remove sexually graphic books and instructional sex guides aimed at children from New Jersey public libraries. Sal Piarulla is a School Board Member who is leading the charge in Garwood to resist and fight back against the Democrat’s plan to sexualize education. Sal is now under fire from the Board of Education, despite winning a decision in court that dismissed the Board's lawsuit against him. Antawn, Fran and Sal are excellent examples of people who are fighting the necessary fight to protect the citizens of New Jersey, including our innocent children who are being victimized by inappropriate indoctrination and sexualization forced into the classrooms. Join us for today's episode and help fight the good fight, Bill PS: If you don't want to miss today's Common Ground LIVE show, == and leave it open until we're live at 4:30PM ET! | | Common Sense Club 165 Route 35 Red Bank, New Jersey 07701 United States

## DEVELOPING BOMBSHELL
## Does UK holding company owned by Phil Murphy have an interest in the global offshore wind industry?
---
---
JECS Limited, a UK company that owns Governor Phil Murphy's Italian mansion shares the same name as a global wind industry service provider, JECS Offshore. Rumors are swirling online this week about the Governor's possible link. We are investigating, but here's what we know so far:

| | Derek, can I tell you a little story about me? I grew up on my family’s farm and it’s still home today. That explains a lot about me. When I was a kid, I lost three fingers on my left hand while working in our family’s butcher shop. I don’t even remember putting my hand there — but I can damn sure remember rushing to a local doctor. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59505270/394407470/-747183808?refcode=mw_em_230302_1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmE4N2ZiNS0wNmI5LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KdLy9h1P64oCa3WsztwO8S8cc52h7YZNxYq62c7eFmA=) Here’s why I’m telling you this: just like my $12 flattop haircut from my local barbershop back home and my collection of dirt-stained t-shirts,**my background is a big part of the reason I remain grounded and focused amid the partisan politics and nonsense happening in our government.** With all the chaos in Washington (especially now), you need somebody who will fight to protect our public lands, create good-paying jobs, and ensure rural communities have hospitals within driving distance in case of an emergency. **I’m pretty different from most people who end up in the U.S. Senate. I’m just a dirt farmer trying to do what’s right for the people in my state and across the country.** **_[**I’m officially running for reelection, so here’s the part where I ask: Can you become a Founding Donor of my campaign to help us block Mitch McConnell from retaking the Senate?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59505271/394407471/-747183808?refcode=mw_em_230302_1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmE4N2ZiNS0wNmI5LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KdLy9h1P64oCa3WsztwO8S8cc52h7YZNxYq62c7eFmA=)_** ****[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59505272/394407472/-747183808?refcode=mw_em_230302_1&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmE4N2ZiNS0wNmI5LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KdLy9h1P64oCa3WsztwO8S8cc52h7YZNxYq62c7eFmA=)**** You don’t need ten fingers to know right from wrong and you can bet I’m going to spend every single day trying to do right by working families, farmers, students, and veterans here in Montana and across the country. I appreciate your support, and anything you can pitch in today will go a long way for our grassroots campaign. Thanks again, Jon ** ** ** ** ****[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59505273/394407473/-747183808?refcode=mw_em_230302_1&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmE4N2ZiNS0wNmI5LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KdLy9h1P64oCa3WsztwO8S8cc52h7YZNxYq62c7eFmA=)**** ** **

name: Greater Georgia
subject: KELLY LOEFFLER APPLAUDS LATEST STACEY ABRAMS INVESTIGATION
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greater Georgia Action, Inc
body:
| | | |
---
| FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE THURSDAY, MARCH 2
KELLY LOEFFLER APPLAUDS LATEST STACEY ABRAMS INVESTIGATION
ATLANTA - Today, former U.S. Senator and Greater Georgia Chairwoman Kelly Loeffler applauded state officials for launching a [new investigation](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0zZPjEogU_DhgZPkjd6zqqNvafYo0x3Og6wGk-0BZJLpAJfmdGfadf7Cbd3ATtUbQTKZOIQpnD6CbNQNXzYLy7ZMyFpHIsx0rLq0Q1eaLO5Dlj11tpxzjeCdZm7FhYogEbfI0CWYm0gLIUR9N2aeWjIL8klcJ9XI-YrIYXLGIsD4xH0dWQxdMjShLrx9KeyPHEsHASsI2yP80nnXv0JCS3SV3bTxMW1kCMDXU6Ij9bU2VCOyDSEJgSboDv7XZImmMm&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==) into Stacey Abrams' New Georgia Project (NGP) following extensive reports about the group's financial impropriety.
"The web of political 'voting rights' organizations built to fuel Stacey Abrams' celebrity and fundraising is under needed scrutiny. Not only did these groups invest millions to perpetuate the voter suppression myth, but they did so using apparently illegal tactics," said Loeffler. "These allegations impair the work of every legitimate organization working honestly and legally to empower and protect Georgia voters. This is why I applaud our state's oversight agencies for taking action to investigate fraudulent front groups in Georgia, before they can interfere in our next election cycle."
Greater Georgia initially [called for an investigation](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0zGYRdPxmld57cTengZrKkpU0nYAxxcRJKsCvmMsngJHsgC5JlGgxb-185tujp7cLlH0WvgQ4AnzKuDhGJ2JKWam0ZVo_8XjUZ34fFqySFtYCxglsjZjTLPT6OdQTQIxDzMZ981oJmwu1oUPndiBTUrvHDo6HEVv0czv_PLBrAV_qWkiBxRdABFw==&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==) into NGP last November after top leaders within the organization accused it of gross financial misconduct. Since then, further reporting by the Washington Free Beacon has revealed a deluge of potentially criminal behavior by NGP, including [failure to file](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0zkqfRhrO-IRqFYdUWP79ZzX8yb7D_8e8G-nnqLqt8UF5TE5tKm64Y1tZjHDdvr3vtpaxMacAREWToq-gR0PHqB0YRiK32ewS_seX560hFzfUSYsxNPL5aaYiEZj8PpRXHJ3D2wXAxZNJB79HaEJT8_foGwStazaGxzJhjytm4OBj5ky8EjkOdPBWI0nUC_b-k&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==) financial disclosures with the IRS, [illegal fundraising](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0zkqfRhrO-IRqFYdUWP79ZzX8yb7D_8e8G-nnqLqt8UF5TE5tKm64Y1tZjHDdvr3vtpaxMacAREWToq-gR0PHqB0YRiK32ewS_seX560hFzfUSYsxNPL5aaYiEZj8PpRXHJ3D2wXAxZNJB79HaEJT8_foGwStazaGxzJhjytm4OBj5ky8EjkOdPBWI0nUC_b-k&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==), and [lying](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0za8sEuu4VlLosAnWyXZxnyqhpZR2pQllh0mF20JQBJQbQ7lW6wlq5r9iJ4F1JgqsVXniyv46rZc1LHTyrrwY_XLLwSRyczOFNd7xV6HNBRauQrYY68r6QiFitDWZyc719qnQCOX1BCw99RMCB7Z_Ziu4EeXmDrY9uNGdFGNPShnvWL65rGcMoW3fWTw4hNH9i&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==) about contributions and expenditures.
Although this latest investigation has been launched by the Georgia Secretary of State's office, NGP has been under investigation for malfeasance by the State Ethics Commission since 2018, when it [illegally campaigned](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pETdlG_uy9Jf2nv34y1aJVkpAozO_k8bVE0BPBPnuPWST9YSoqwQ09iHCVn140UBcs6d37_EyY3Lfq3ZNIc2Y7r32BBfjPD_iPX_T4v4JZlO6ra-2LAwWHS2GFXXId1_nytVsXbyEGD0s9A0O4hw4ZyX-XbbeBGeVVOzQviMi5imM9WtYD787YGRYYXlRs5WFFPSoUzdkTO_NP0-7jV3G1F8KKndu_yVFw==&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==) for Abrams and refused to disclose more than $4 million in contributions to her campaign. Abrams herself has faced financial challenges in recent months, disclosing [almost $1.5 million in debt ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001yI8Krxf09rhUjVk6JGYXAcUUpmc3jR1AdXHD789v9-sqwuHe-_K3pLGyaBfZ2L0zMiC0hVZ7ZAOetjVDKaTljsvuKiEDulsQZ-PPId3rQMjc2Fxke3IlVRZPT18sX82NkWFfdwlcT2JriD0Mp2ND41a-eNjctYpccFqgWEMwhU2_b-yZxoCsZpC8NwU2HKmv&c=H4y_mPSYkpy1DrDcSES6RPtEdThOihUqGU9L7cYGBT5aXUI_45koFA==&ch=-Z6JMBFgEOQ34PpJScekgcNck3iGRaIG2ikg_Nrb6RE9bRkIf-WmlA==)from her failed gubernatorial campaign in 2022, despite raising well over $100 million for the race.
Greater Georgia, launched by former U.S. Senator Kelly Loeffler in 2021, is a 501(c4) nonpartisan nonprofit dedicated to growing participation in the democratic process by mobilizing and empowering voters. The group works year-round to educate and register voters, engage with diverse and underrepresented communities, and protect election integrity.

clean_body: | | | | | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE THURSDAY, MARCH 2 KELLY LOEFFLER APPLAUDS LATEST STACEY ABRAMS INVESTIGATION ATLANTA - Today, former U.S. Senator and Greater Georgia Chairwoman Kelly Loeffler applauded state officials for launching a == into Stacey Abrams' New Georgia Project (NGP) following extensive reports about the group's financial impropriety. "The web of political 'voting rights' organizations built to fuel Stacey Abrams' celebrity and fundraising is under needed scrutiny. Not only did these groups invest millions to perpetuate the voter suppression myth, but they did so using apparently illegal tactics," said Loeffler. "These allegations impair the work of every legitimate organization working honestly and legally to empower and protect Georgia voters. This is why I applaud our state's oversight agencies for taking action to investigate fraudulent front groups in Georgia, before they can interfere in our next election cycle." Greater Georgia initially == into NGP last November after top leaders within the organization accused it of gross financial misconduct. Since then, further reporting by the Washington Free Beacon has revealed a deluge of potentially criminal behavior by NGP, including == financial disclosures with the IRS, ==, and == about contributions and expenditures. Although this latest investigation has been launched by the Georgia Secretary of State's office, NGP has been under investigation for malfeasance by the State Ethics Commission since 2018, when it == for Abrams and refused to disclose more than $4 million in contributions to her campaign. Abrams herself has faced financial challenges in recent months, disclosing her failed gubernatorial campaign in 2022, despite raising well over $100 million for the race. subject: Thank you

body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.

Team,

The road to flipping our House blue starts with you — and that’s why I want to start this month by saying thank you!

Thanks to your support, we hit our end-of-month fundraising goal and are on track to do what it takes to win big in 2024.

Because of you, I’m able to run our campaign the right way — I’m able to stand up and fight for a government that works for the people, not MAGA millionaires or interest groups. I’m proud to ensure that the needs of our community are put first every step of the way, each and every day.

If you want to make a difference, you need a great team — and by coming together once more to support our campaign, you’ve shown that we’ve got the team to make it happen.

Thank you for standing with me,

Eric Swalwell

name: Nikki Haley
subject: As a mom
body:
I understand what it's like to be a mom in an economy that is failing so many of us
| |
---
| | | | [](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r)
---
| | |
Peter,

I have a daughter who is getting married and I see how hard it is for her to buy a home and I have a son who has to write papers that he doesn't believe in just to get an A.

I understand what it's like to be a mom in an economy that is failing so many of us, and I understand what it's like to put 1,000% into everything, even when faced with the most difficult times and impossible decisions. _**I know so many of you understand this too.**_
---
| | | [](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r)
---
I've watched the American Dream unfold before me. I've seen that even on our worst days, we are blessed to live in America. But I also know that under Biden's leadership, times are tough and America can be doing so much better.

**[I'm running for President because America can do better, and I know how.](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r "I’m running for President because America can do better, and I know how. ")**

As governor, I slashed our unemployment rate, brought jobs to our state, and shepherded in a successful economic recovery.

At the UN I fought for America, made sure our allies knew we had their backs, stood up for Israel even when others refused, and stood up to murderers and dictators to let them know that America was taking names.

I know how to make America strong and proud because I've done it before.**[And I'll do it as your next President.](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r " And I’ll do it as your next President.")**

We can revitalize the economy, support small businesses, stop Washington's spending sprees, protect our border, champion school choice, and save America's future.

In Biden's economy, small businesses are struggling to pay rent, families are struggling to pay for groceries, and I know not everyone can afford to chip in right now. **[So if you are someone who can donate, I am humbly asking to give everything you are able to help fuel my efforts and fight for a brighter future.](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r "So if you are
someone who can donate, I am humbly asking to give everything you are able to help fuel my efforts and fight for a brighter future. ")**
---

My very best,

---
| [](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62382871:J3iGa3GNj:m:1:3365131770:F5BA8BC60D3571CFC879F430983ACF9A:r)
---
Nikki Haley
---
| | | | |
---
---
| |

Nikki Haley is the daughter of Indian immigrants, a proud military wife, and the mom of two amazing children. She's served as governor of the great state of South Carolina and as America's ambassador to the United Nations...and she's a grateful citizen who knows our best days are yet to come, if we unite and fight to save our country.

Nikki Haley is running for President because our country is on a path of doubt, division, and self-destruction, and we need a leader who has proven time and again that she can rise to the occasion, offer solutions, and focus on serving the people.

clean_body: I understand what it's like to be a mom in an economy that is failing so many of us | | | | | | | | | Peter, I have a daughter who is getting married and I see how hard it is for her to buy a home and I have a son who has to write papers that he doesn't believe in just to get an A. I understand what it's like to be a mom in an economy that is failing so many of us, and I understand what it's like to put 1,000% into everything, even when faced with the most difficult times and impossible decisions. I know so many of you understand this too. | | | I've watched the American Dream unfold before me. I've seen that even on our worst days, we are blessed to live in America. But I also know that under Biden's leadership, times are tough and America can be doing so much better. "I’m running for President because America can do better, and I know how. " As governor, I slashed our unemployment rate, brought jobs to our state, and shepherded in a successful economic recovery. At the UN I fought for America, made sure our allies knew we had their backs, stood up for Israel even when others refused, and stood up to murderers and dictators to let them know that America was taking names. I know how to make America strong and proud because I've done it before. " And I’ll do it as your next President." We can revitalize the economy, support small businesses, stop Washington's spending sprees, protect our border, champion school choice, and save America's future. In Biden's economy, small businesses are struggling to pay rent, families are struggling to pay for groceries, and I know not everyone can afford to chip in right now. "So if you are someone who can donate, I am humbly asking to give everything you are able to help fuel my efforts and fight for a brighter future. name: Rep. Dana Criswell
subject: Support Steve Hopkins for Senate
body:
|
---
| | |
---
| Mississippi’s children are now protected from dangerous gender transition procedures and surgeries!

I am proud to have coauthored HB 1125, the Regulate Experimental Adolescent Procedures (REAP) Act, which has been signed into law this week by Gov. Tate Reeves and will go into effect immediately.
---
| | |
---
| | [JOIN OUR FIGHT TO PROTECT MISSISSIPPI'S CHILDREN WITH A GENEROUS GRASSROOTS DONATION TODAY >>> ](https://www.votervoice.net/BroadcastLinks/J0ze6bLmXATsC1IQxyloRg)
---
| As a father and grandfather, I know firsthand how important it is to protect our children from the radical left's dangerous attack on our children. As your elected representative, I am committed to protecting all of Mississippi’s children from the socialists' agenda.

The REAP Act prohibits healthcare professionals from providing hormone treatments and surgery to minors and makes it illegal for people to “knowingly engage in conduct that aids or abets the performance or inducement of gender transition procedures” to minors.

_**Any healthcare professionals found to be in violation of the ban will have their license to practice medicine in the state revoked.**_

When I am elected to the Senate, I promise that I will continue to fight for Mississippi's children and for the values of the families of our great state. With your support, we will end the unprecedented attacks on our children and families. Please partner with me in this fight by making a campaign contribution today. [**Can I count on your support ?**](https://www.votervoice.net/BroadcastLinks/bNwOGH270vZ1t_k2C4GitA)
---
| | |
---
| Steve Hopkins
Candidate for State Senate District 1
Personal Cell: (901) 827-0755
---
| You are receiving this email because you signed up for alerts from Mississippi Freedom Caucus.
Click [here](https://www.votervoice.net/BroadcastLinks/nhxQGukYHWUHzYI-JKnjxw) to unsubscribe from this mailing list.

---
| | [ ](https://www.votervoice.net)
---
